findings.detail: ATLAS reported GI=0.83/green at 17:01Z; independent check shows GI=0.73/yellow at 17:35Z. 0.10 drop in 34min.
source=kv (not mock), but seed_result=401 indicates KV seed auth is broken. Data is from KV cache, not fresh live computation.
Freshness signal at 0.30 — primary drag on GI. Multiple stale data feeds detected.
2/25 microagents unhealthy (ZEUS-µ3: no signal, JADE-µ2: no signal). 3 agents on watch severity (ATLAS-µ1, HERMES-µ3, HERMES-µ4).
5 of 10 KV keys absent: SIGNAL_SNAPSHOT, TRIPWIRE_STATE, TRIPWIRE_STATE_KV, SYSTEM_PULSE, TRIPWIRE_STATE_REDIS.
0 candidates found — no verification required this cycle.
POST /api/agents/journal returned 401 — CRON_SECRET not available in execution environment.

recommendation: Operator attention needed: (1) Fix CRON_SECRET/auth configuration causing persistent 401 on self-ping, KV seed, and agent journal writes. (2) Investigate freshness signal degradation (0.30). (3) Triage persistent microagent failures (ZEUS-µ3, JADE-µ2). GI is not trustworthy at reported level — actual integrity is lower than ATLAS captured.
